texto: Never stop reading, Luke. The words from the pages make me feel like I'm right there, like I can smell the sea and hear the wind. It's my one regret that I didn't read much over the years. How can you learn about all the things happening in the world if you don't read?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Lea Davey: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Never stop reading, Luke. The words from the pages make me feel like I'm right there, like I can …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, reading-books, never pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

frase_original: Never stop reading, Luke. The words from the pages make me feel like I'm right there, like I can smell the sea and hear the wind. It's my one regret that I didn't read much over the years. How can you learn about all the things happening in the world if you don't read?

texto: Where have the years gone, Ruby Rose? Sometimes I have to stop and think about how old I am. When I wake up in the morning, before I move this tired old body or look in the blasted mirror, I swear I'm still a young man. It just feels like yesterday. I don't know how it's gone so fast.

frase_original: Where have the years gone, Ruby Rose? Sometimes I have to stop and think about how old I am. When I wake up in the morning, before I move this tired old body or look in the blasted mirror, I swear I'm still a young man. It just feels like yesterday. I don't know how it's gone so fast.
